nameMeanings.name: Bauer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Erwin, Fritz, Helmut, Heinz, Manfred, Franz, Gerhard, Johannes, Wolfgang.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): status name for a peasant or nickname meaning ‘neighbor, fellow citizen’, from Middle High German (ge)būr, Middle Low German būr, denoting an occupant of a būr, a small dwelling or building. This word later fell together with Middle High German būwære, an agent noun from Old High German būwan ‘to cultivate’, later also (at first in Low German dialects) ‘to build’. The precise meaning of the Jewish surname, which is of later formation, is unclear. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), the Netherlands, Denmark, Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic status names or surnames.

variants.name: Agricola
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Algert, Cornelis.  German Aloys, Otto.
German and Dutch: humanistic surname, a translation into Latin of German   Bauer   and Dutch   Boer   ‘farmer’.

variants.name: Bauerlein
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Gunther.
German (Bäuerlein): standardized diminutive of Bauer , originally occurring in southern dialects as Bäuerle (see Bauerle ), Beuerle , and Beyerle .

variants.name: Baur
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Uli, Ulrich, Alfons, Arno, Erwin, Friedrich, Fritz, Urs, Wilhelm, Wolfgang.
South German: variant of   Bauer   ‘peasant’ or ‘neighbor, fellow citizen’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Ankenbauer
meanings: South German (mainly Bavaria): from Middle High German anken ‘butter’ + būr ‘farmer’ (see Bauer ), hence an occupational name for a dairy farmer. Alternatively, the first element may be a topographic term, Anke, meaning ‘slight rise in the ground’, or an old term ank ‘watery’, hence ‘farmer with fields in a wet area’.

variants.name: Bierle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto.
South German: from a diminutive of Bauer , an unrounded form of Alemannic Bürle (compare Buehrle ).

variants.name: Beurer
meanings: South German: habitational name for someone from any of various places called Beuren, from Old High German būr ‘small dwelling’ (see Bauer ).

variants.name: Bauerle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Albrecht, Helmut, Horst, Lothar, Merwin, Otto.
South German (Bäuerle): from a Swabian diminutive of Bauer .

variants.name: Bauers
meanings: German: variant of Bauer , with post-medieval excrescent -s, or from a first part of an original compound name (e.g. Bauersfeld ).

variants.name: Beuerle
meanings: South German: diminutive of   Bauer  .

variants.name: Baumann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Erwin, Otto, Gerhard, Ernst, Heinz, Guenter, Armin, Dieter, Erna, Friedrich.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): status name for a peasant or a nickname meaning ‘neighbor, fellow citizen’, from Middle High German būman, German Baumann ‘peasant’ (see Bauer ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), the Netherlands, Denmark, Poland, and some other European countries. Compare Bauman and Bowman .

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: Burwood
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Burghwood Farm in Ormesby Saint Michael or Burwood Hall in Mileham (both Norfolk).

variants.name: Barwood
meanings: English:  see  Burwood  .
